page: Amazon Web Services c8in.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU resources are fully dedicated, ensuring consistent compute performance without resource contention. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. It is network and EBS optimized, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8in.2xlarge provides a dense compute profile with a low memory-to-core ratio, making it a cost-effective option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local scratch disks. Tradeoffs include the complete reliance on network-attached storage and the lack of hardware acceleration. This configuration is suitable for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and compute-intensive application servers.
